agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"POST /validate","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"Single external dependency with no fallback or circuit breaker. If SmartyStreets degrades (slow but not timeout), all inbound requests queue up and exhaust connection pool."},{"from":"/health","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"Health check couples liveness to an external dependency. If SmartyStreets has a brief outage, orchestrator (k8s) may kill healthy pods, causing a cascading self-inflicted outage. Liveness should be internal-only; SmartyStreets reachability belongs on a separate readiness probe."}],"contract_compliance":"No validation of SmartyStreets response schema. If they change payload shape, the service will throw unhandled parse errors with no actionable log.","data_flow_integrity":"Input address is not logged and raw API response is not logged, making it impossible to reproduce or audit a specific validation decision after the fact."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No request ID / correlation ID in any log line — cannot trace a single request across log aggregator","No input address logged — cannot reproduce failures or answer 'why was my address rejected?'","No raw SmartyStreets response logged (even at DEBUG) — cannot diagnose upstream behavior changes","No duration of the SmartyStreets call logged — cannot distinguish slow upstream from slow service","No structured logging apparent — grep-ability and dashboard parsing will be fragile"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"minimal — single counter with no dimensional breakdown","gaps":["validation_requests_total lacks result label (valid/invalid/error) — cannot alert on spike in invalid or error results","No latency histogram (e.g. validation_duration_seconds) — cannot set SLO or detect upstream degradation","No in-flight request gauge — cannot detect connection pool exhaustion","No error counter by type (timeout, 5xx, parse error) — cannot distinguish failure modes in dashboards","No SmartyStreets availability metric — cannot correlate service errors with upstream outages"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed trace context propagation — if called from another service, the trace breaks here","No span for the outbound SmartyStreets HTTP call — invisible in trace waterfall"]},"debugging_capability":"Poor. An on-call engineer at 3am receiving a 'validation errors spiking' alert has no request ID to grep, no input to reproduce, no upstream latency to inspect, and no error breakdown to isolate root cause. They would be flying blind."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"SmartyStreets returns 5xx errors","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Implement retry with exponential backoff (1 retry, jittered) for 5xx/network errors only. Log each retry attempt with request ID."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets responds slowly (2.5s) but under timeout","handling":"partial","recommendation":"3s timeout exists but no latency alarm. Add P99 latency alert at 2s so you catch degradation before it hits timeout. Consider adaptive timeout or deadline propagation from caller."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets is fully down for extended period","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a circuit breaker (e.g. 50% error rate over 30s window → open). When open, return a clear 503 with Retry-After header instead of burning all timeout budgets. Optionally return a degraded response indicating validation is temporarily unavailable."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets changes response schema","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Validate response shape before processing. On parse failure, log the raw response at ERROR with request ID and return 502."},{"scenario":"Burst of inbound traffic","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add rate limiting or max-concurrency guard to prevent overwhelming the SmartyStreets API quota and protect the service's own resources."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets API key expiry or quota exceeded","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Detect 401/403/429 specifically, log as CRITICAL with distinct error codes, and expose a metric so alerting fires immediately rather than showing as generic errors."}],"retry_config":"None configured. At least one retry on 5xx/network errors with jitter is recommended.","timeout_config":"3s timeout exists — reasonable starting point. Needs a corresponding P99 latency alert well below the timeout (e.g. 2s) to catch degradation early.","circuit_breakers":"None. Required before production to prevent cascade failure during sustained SmartyStreets outage.","graceful_degradation":"None. Service has no defined behavior when its sole dependency is unavailable. Should return 503 with structured error body so callers can degrade gracefully on their side."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous container image. Acceptable for a stateless service with no database migrations. Verify image tag pinning so 'previous' is deterministic.","monitoring_coverage":"Insufficient. A single counter without labels provides almost no operational signal. Cannot alert on error rate, latency degradation, or upstream availability.","on_call_documentation":"Not mentioned and likely missing. Need a runbook covering: (1) how to verify SmartyStreets status, (2) how to rotate API keys, (3) what to do when error rate spikes, (4) how to manually validate an address for debugging, (5) escalation contacts at SmartyStreets.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add Prometheus histogram validation_duration_seconds","Add result label to validation_requests_total","Add circuit breaker configuration (error threshold, window, recovery timeout)","Split /health into liveness (internal) and readiness (SmartyStreets) probes"],"infra_provisioning":["Grafana dashboard for the service","PagerDuty/alert rules for error rate and P99 latency"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"Stateless service, no database involved."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No correlation/request ID, no latency histogram, no error breakdown metric, no tracing","reason":"On-call engineer cannot diagnose any production incident with current instrumentation. This is the highest-priority fix — every other improvement depends on being able to see what is happening.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No circuit breaker or retry logic for sole external dependency","reason":"A sustained SmartyStreets outage will cause 100% of requests to hang for 3s then fail, exhausting connection pools and potentially cascading upstream. Circuit breaker is a must-have for a service whose entire value depends on one external call.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Health check liveness probe depends on external service","reason":"Kubernetes liveness probe hitting SmartyStreets means an upstream blip triggers pod restarts, turning a partial outage into a full self-inflicted outage. Must separate liveness from readiness.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Observability gaps are clear and unambiguous","Circuit breaker / resilience gap is a textbook concern for single-dependency services","Health check anti-pattern is well-documented"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact retry tuning depends on SmartyStreets SLA and rate limits not provided","Whether callers already handle 503 gracefully is unknown"],"assumptions":["Service is deployed on Kubernetes or similar orchestrator with liveness/readiness probe distinction","SmartyStreets is the only external dependency","No caching layer sits between the service and SmartyStreets","Service is stateless with no database"]}}
